evals.prompt: Create a LinkedIn hat for me. (assumes populated SOUL)
evals.expected_output: Skill verifies SOUL is populated, looks up LinkedIn syntax from built-in reference (no markdown, 3000 char limit, autolinks only, etc.), asks 9 personal questions, drafts a hat with Platform Syntax + Target Channels + Tone Overlay + Post Structure + Hook Formulas + Hard Limits + Failure Modes + AI Detection Checklist + Required Structural Rules + Workflow + clipboard step.

assertions.text: Skill verifies SOUL is populated before proceeding
Skill pre-fills LinkedIn syntax from built-in reference, does NOT ask user about syntax
Skill asks personal questions (failure modes, tone, channels, hard limits, structure, hook formulas, AI detection)
Generated hat includes Platform Syntax section
Generated hat includes Hard Limits section
Generated hat includes AI Detection Checklist with [ ] items
Generated hat references soul as base layer
Generated hat includes clipboard step in workflow

evals.prompt: Create a hat for an obscure platform: BlueWidget Forum. (assumes populated SOUL)
evals.expected_output: Skill falls back to asking the user about syntax (since BlueWidget Forum is not in the built-in reference). All other personal questions are asked normally.

assertions.text: Skill detects platform not in reference and asks syntax questions
Skill does not fabricate syntax for unknown platform

evals.prompt: Create a Reddit hat for me. (SOUL is unpopulated)
evals.expected_output: Skill detects SOUL is unpopulated and refuses to proceed. Routes user to /soul.

assertions.text: Skill refuses to derive when SOUL is unpopulated
Skill points user at /soul or the onboarding flow
